title: Sarajevo Tourism Report 2024
extracted_text: Sarajevo welcomed over 1.2 million tourists in 2024, a 15% increase from the previous year. The Old Town (Baščaršija) remains the most visited area, followed by the Trebević cable car and the War Tunnel Museum. Hotel occupancy rates averaged 72% during summer months. International visitors primarily came from Turkey, Croatia, Serbia, and Germany. The city's investment in cultural events, including the Sarajevo Film Festival, contributed significantly to tourism growth.

title: Quarterly Sales Summary Q3
extracted_text: Third quarter revenue reached 2.4 million KM, exceeding targets by 8%. The technology division led growth with a 23% increase in software licensing. Hardware sales declined by 5% due to supply chain disruptions. Customer acquisition cost decreased to 145 KM per customer, down from 180 KM in Q2. The sales team expanded to 12 members with two new hires in September.

title: Annual Budget Proposal 2025
extracted_text: The proposed budget for fiscal year 2025 allocates 1.8 million KM to technology infrastructure, representing a 20% increase from 2024. Key investments include cloud migration (400,000 KM), cybersecurity improvements (250,000 KM), and new development tools and licenses (150,000 KM). Personnel costs account for 60% of the total IT budget. The proposal recommends hiring three additional developers and one data engineer.

title: Research Paper Draft - NLP
extracted_text: This paper presents a novel approach to sentiment analysis for Bosnian language text using transformer-based models. We fine-tuned a multilingual BERT model on a corpus of 50,000 annotated social media posts. Results show 87% accuracy on the test set, outperforming previous rule-based approaches by 12 percentage points. The model demonstrates particular strength in detecting sarcasm and implicit sentiment in informal text. Future work will extend the approach to aspect-based sentiment analysis.

GDPR compliance confirmed.

title: lecture_recording_week4
extracted_text: Today we are going to talk about document extraction. This is one of the most common tasks in unstructured data processing. When you receive a PDF file, you need to extract the text content from it. There are several Python libraries that can help: pdfplumber is great for text and tables, PyMuPDF is faster for large files, and python-docx handles Word documents. The key challenge is handling different PDF formats - some have selectable text while others are just scanned images requiring OCR.

title: interview_student_project
extracted_text: I chose to build a news aggregator for Bosnian media as my project. The pipeline collects articles from klix.ba, avaz.ba, and oslobodjenje.ba using web scraping. Then I extract the main article text, clean it, and store it in MongoDB with metadata. The most interesting part was implementing sentiment analysis for Bosnian text because there are very few pre-trained models available. I ended up fine-tuning a multilingual model and got decent results.

title: podcast_episode_12_ai
extracted_text: The rise of large language models has completely changed how we think about unstructured data. Previously, extracting meaning from text, images, and audio required separate specialized models. Now, multimodal models can process all these data types simultaneously. However, for production systems, specialized pipelines still offer better accuracy and lower costs. The sweet spot is using embeddings to bridge different data types into a common vector space for unified search.

Follow up with the programme committee next week.

title: klix.ba - Razvoj IT sektora u BiH
extracted_text: Informaciono-tehnološki sektor u Bosni i Hercegovini bilježi kontinuiran rast. Prema najnovijim podacima, IT industrija zapošljava preko 15,000 radnika i ostvaruje godišnji promet od preko 500 miliona KM. Glavni centri razvoja su Sarajevo, Banja Luka i Mostar. Outsourcing usluge čine 60% ukupnog prihoda sektora. Vlada FBiH najavila je porezne olakšice za IT kompanije u narednom periodu.

title: hackernews - Discussion on Vector Databases
extracted_text: The debate between Pinecone, Weaviate, and ChromaDB continues. For small to medium projects, ChromaDB offers the simplest setup - you can embed it directly in your Python application without running a separate server. Weaviate shines for multi-modal search with built-in vectorization. Pinecone is the managed option if you don't want to deal with infrastructure. For learning purposes, start with ChromaDB and move to managed solutions when you need scale.

title: Wikipedia - Bosnia and Herzegovina Education
extracted_text: Bosnia and Herzegovina has 8 public universities and several private universities. The higher education system follows the Bologna Process with three cycles: bachelor (3-4 years), master (1-2 years), and doctoral studies. International Burch University, founded in 2008, is a private university in Sarajevo offering programs in engineering, natural sciences, and social sciences. The university has partnerships with institutions across Europe and offers instruction in English.

title: balkaninsight - Digital Transformation Report
extracted_text: Western Balkan countries are accelerating digital transformation efforts. Bosnia and Herzegovina launched its e-Government portal in 2024, enabling citizens to access 50 public services online. Serbia leads the region with 85% of government services digitized. North Macedonia introduced digital signatures for all official documents. The EU's Digital Agenda for the Western Balkans provides funding for IT infrastructure improvements, with 200 million EUR allocated for 2024-2027.

title: avaz.ba - Sajam zapošljavanja Sarajevo

Prosječna plata za juniore iznosi 1,800 KM, za seniore 4,500 KM. Kompanije Mistral, Klika i Atlantic Grupa ponudile su stipendije za studente završnih godina. Organizator najavljuje naredni sajam u martu 2025.

title: Server Migration Plan
extracted_text: Phase 1: Inventory all current servers and services (2 weeks). Phase 2: Set up cloud infrastructure on Azure (1 week). Phase 3: Migrate non-critical services first - email, file storage, internal wiki (2 weeks). Phase 4: Migrate production databases with zero-downtime strategy using read replicas (1 week). Phase 5: Migrate application servers with blue-green deployment (1 week). Phase 6: Decommission old hardware (1 week). Total timeline: 8 weeks. Risk mitigation: maintain parallel systems for 30 days after migration.

title: Security Audit Report October 2024
extracted_text: Critical findings: 1) SQL injection vulnerability in the student portal login form - CVSS score 9.1. 2) Outdated SSL certificates on three internal servers. 3) Default credentials found on the development database server. Medium findings: 4) Missing rate limiting on API endpoints. 5) Insufficient logging on authentication failures. 6) No encryption for backup files stored on network drives. Recommendations: immediate patching of critical vulnerabilities, implementation of WAF, and mandatory security training for all developers.

Areas for improvement: more examples of real-world applications (mentioned by 35% of students), additional office hours (20%), slower pace in weeks covering embeddings (15%). Student comments highlight the pipeline project as the most valuable learning experience.

title: lecture_recording_embeddings
extracted_text: Let me explain how embeddings work. Think of it this way - every word or sentence gets converted into a list of numbers, a vector. Similar meanings produce similar vectors. So the word car and the word automobile would have very similar vectors, even though the letters are completely different. This is what makes semantic search possible. Instead of matching exact keywords, we compare the meaning of the query with the meaning of our documents. The sentence transformers library makes this very easy in Python.

title: student_presentation_project
extracted_text: For my semester project I built a recipe search engine for Bosnian cuisine. I collected 500 recipes from web scraping, extracted ingredients and instructions, generated embeddings using a multilingual model, and stored everything in ChromaDB. Users can search by describing what they want to cook, like 'something warm for winter with meat' and the system returns relevant recipes even if those exact words don't appear in the recipe text. The accuracy was 82% based on manual evaluation.

title: Medium - Building Semantic Search with Python
extracted_text: Building a semantic search engine is simpler than you think. Step 1: Choose your embedding model. For English text, all-MiniLM-L6-v2 offers a great balance of speed and quality. For multilingual needs, paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2 supports 50+ languages. Step 2: Generate embeddings for your document collection. Step 3: Store them in a vector database like ChromaDB or FAISS. Step 4: At query time, embed the user's question and find the nearest neighbors. The whole pipeline can be built in under 50 lines of Python code.

title: Dev.to - Elasticsearch vs Vector Search
extracted_text: Elasticsearch excels at full-text search with features like fuzzy matching, stemming, and relevance scoring using BM25. Vector databases like ChromaDB are better for semantic understanding - finding documents that mean the same thing even with different words. The best approach? Use both. Hybrid search combines keyword matching for exact terms (product codes, names, dates) with vector similarity for conceptual queries. In production, we use Elasticsearch for structured queries and ChromaDB for semantic fallback.

title: blog - ChromaDB Production Tips
extracted_text: After running ChromaDB in production for 6 months with 2 million documents, here are my tips: 1) Always use PersistentClient for data that matters. 2) Batch your inserts - adding documents one by one is 10x slower. 3) Use metadata filtering before vector search to narrow the search space. 4) Choose your distance metric carefully - cosine for normalized embeddings, L2 for unnormalized. 5) Monitor collection size - performance degrades above 5 million documents without sharding.

title: Python Workshop Handout
extracted_text: Introduction to Python for data processing. Python is the most popular language for data science and machine learning. Key libraries: pandas for tabular data, NumPy for numerical computing, matplotlib for visualization, scikit-learn for machine learning. Environment setup: install Anaconda or use pip with virtual environments. Best practices: use type hints, write docstrings, follow PEP 8 style guide, test your code with pytest.

title: Database Comparison Report
extracted_text: Comparison of database solutions for the document management system. PostgreSQL: strong ACID compliance, excellent for structured data, supports JSON columns. MongoDB: flexible schema, good for documents with varying structure, built-in text search. ChromaDB: purpose-built for vector similarity search, integrates with embedding models. Recommendation: use PostgreSQL for user accounts and metadata, MongoDB for raw documents, ChromaDB for semantic search. This polyglot approach leverages each database's strengths.

title: klix.ba - IBU na listi najboljih univerziteta
extracted_text: International Burch University ponovo se našao na listi najboljih univerziteta u regionu prema Times Higher Education rangiranju. Univerzitet se posebno ističe u oblasti inženjerstva i informacijskih tehnologija. Rektor je izjavio da univerzitet nastavlja ulagati u istraživačke kapacitete i međunarodnu saradnju. U akademskoj godini 2024/2025 upisano je rekordnih 800 studenata na sve studijske programe.

title: architecture_diagram_photo
extracted_text: System Architecture: Client Layer (React frontend, mobile app) connects to API Gateway (FastAPI). API Gateway connects to three services: Document Service (handles upload, extraction, storage), Search Service (manages embeddings, vector search, full-text search), and Analytics Service (generates reports, visualizations). All services connect to shared data stores: PostgreSQL, MongoDB, ChromaDB, MinIO for file storage.

title: oslobodjenje.ba - Digitalizacija obrazovanja
extracted_text: Ministarstvo obrazovanja KS pokrenulo je projekat digitalizacije obrazovnog sistema. Svi univerziteti u Kantonu Sarajevo trebaju implementirati sisteme za e-učenje do kraja 2025. godine. Projekat uključuje nabavku opreme, obuku nastavnog osoblja i razvoj digitalnih obrazovnih sadržaja. Za projekat je izdvojeno 5 miliona KM iz budžeta Kantona.

title: Docker Workshop Guide
extracted_text: Getting started with Docker for Python applications. A Dockerfile defines your application environment. Start with FROM python:3.11-slim as the base image. Copy your requirements.txt and run pip install. Copy your application code. Expose the necessary ports and define the entry point. Use docker-compose for multi-container setups: your app, database, and search engine each in their own container. Benefits: consistent environments, easy deployment, scalability.

title: Smjernice za pisanje završnog rada
extracted_text: Završni rad na dodiplomskom studiju treba sadržavati sljedeće dijelove: uvod sa motivacijom i ciljevima istraživanja, pregled literature sa minimalno 15 relevantnih izvora, opis metodologije, implementaciju sa primjerima koda, evaluaciju rezultata, zaključak i preporuke. Rad treba biti napisan na engleskom jeziku, minimalno 30 stranica bez priloga. Korištenje AI alata je dozvoljeno uz obavezno navođenje.
